name: Antigravity IDA Bridge
description: AI agent interface to IDA Pro reverse engineering environment

auth.note: Token is auto-generated when IDA plugin starts. Read from file and send as Authorization: Bearer <token>

ide_agent.description: For AI IDE agents (Antigravity IDE, Cursor, Windsurf). The IDE agent IS the brain — it reads this config and calls bridge.py via shell commands.

mcp_server.description: For MCP-compatible clients (Claude Desktop, Cursor, Cline). Exposes 45 native MCP tools.

standalone_agent.description: Interactive agent with built-in AI. Supports 5 backends.

direct_api.description: Raw REST API. Any HTTP client can call endpoints directly.

script_execution.description: The agent can generate arbitrary Python scripts and execute them inside IDA Pro in real-time. This is the most powerful tool — it gives full access to the IDA SDK.
script_execution.return_data: Place results in the global 'result' dict: result['key'] = value
script_execution.stdout_capture: print() output is captured and returned in response

examples.task: Get all function names and addresses
examples.script:
import idautils, idc
result['functions'] = [{'ea': hex(ea), 'name': idc.get_func_name(ea)} for ea in idautils.Functions()]

examples.task: Find all calls to a specific API
Rename all sub_ functions based on string references
Dump decompiled pseudocode of current function
examples.script:
import ida_hexrays
cfunc = ida_hexrays.decompile(idc.here())
result['pseudocode'] = str(cfunc)

examples.task: Map all imported DLLs and their functions

tips: You can write ANY valid Python that would work in IDA's Script Command window
Use idc.set_name(), idc.set_cmt() to annotate the database
Long scripts are fine — there is no size limit
If an endpoint doesn't exist for what you need, generate a script instead

info.description: Binary metadata (name, arch, bits, entry, func count)
